dependencies: Two data suppliers each representing more than 10% of total data costs
One cloud computing supplier provided majority of cloud computing support
Certain data sets have limited number of suppliers
risks: Limited alternative sources for certain data sets
Failure to renew or comply with third-party content agreements
Increased accessibility to free or inexpensive information sources

regulatory_risks: European Union General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
UK data privacy laws including Financial Services and Markets Bill
California Consumer Privacy Act
Connecticut Personal Data Privacy and Online Monitoring Act
China Personal Information Protection Law
India Digital Personal Data Protection Act
Markets in Financial Instruments Directive (MiFID II) in EU and UK
Brexit regulatory uncertainty
Evolving AI regulations
Climate-related disclosure requirements
Cybersecurity regulations
Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA) requirements
SEC reporting and disclosure obligations
Rule 15c3-1 net capital requirements
Tax law changes in multiple jurisdictions
Sales tax compliance (Massachusetts sales tax dispute)
Income tax audits by federal, state and foreign authorities
Inflation Reduction Act excise tax on stock repurchases
OECD Base Erosion and Profit Shifting Pillar Two (15% global minimum tax)
Corporate Alternative Minimum Tax (CAMT)

sanction_risks: Civil disturbances or catastrophic events reducing business activity
Risk that conflicts between Ukraine and Russia and in Middle East expand impacting business operations
